perClaim.text: Observability: Emitting one trace event per agent step lets teams reconstruct any run end to end.
Observability: Per-step tracing is the biggest lever on mean-time-to-diagnose for agent incidents.
Evaluation: A regression gate that blocks releases below an eval pass-rate threshold catches prompt drift.
Evaluation: Treating the golden set as a unit test for the agent surfaces defects before they ship.
Cost: Attributing token usage per agent role makes runaway orchestrator loops visible in the bill.
Cost: A per-role token budget is the cheapest cost guardrail and the first to pay off.
Trust: A groundedness check rejects any synthesized claim that lacks a source reference.
Trust: Requiring a citation per claim stops the agent from asserting facts it cannot cite.

question: What are the load-bearing practices for running reliable AI agents in production?
